name: browser-web-search
versionNotes: 0.4.10 — Residual-risk hardening on the v0.4.4 sealed-tier model. Adds Gate 4 (platform consent ledger at ~/.bws/consents.json bound to (site, pkgVersion, entrySha512)), a structured stderr transparency block on every sensitive call, and a --dry-run launcher flag that runs all four gates + integrity verification without importing the package. Directly responds to the ClawScan May 2026 verdict's residual concern that 'core functionality inherently relies on a third-party dependency to handle sensitive session data'. Underlying npm package browser-web-search remains pinned at 0.4.3 (no change to SHA-512 integrity gate, ENTRY_EXPECTED_SIZE, or symlink rejection).

install.riskReason: 通过 npm 全局安装第三方包，该包会在浏览器页面上下文中执行 JavaScript。安装前请审计源码。

entry.verifyCommand: P=$(npm root -g)/browser-web-search/dist/index.js && shasum -a 512 "$P" | awk '{print $1}' | xxd -r -p | base64 | sed 's|^|sha512-|'

policy.rationale: Decouples per-install trust from manual auditing; mismatch always denies regardless of opt-in flags.

dependencies.note: Package is installed globally via npm and loaded in-process by the launcher (no subprocess). OpenClaw CLI is required by browser-web-search internally.

writeDetails.~/.bws/audit.log: append-only JSON Lines audit log; rotated at 1 MiB
writeDetails.~/.bws/consents.json: v0.4.10 platform consent ledger keyed by site; bound to (pkgVersion, entrySha512)

network.note: 访问支持的平台域名，通过 OpenClaw 浏览器执行

access: 当前页面 Session（继承登录态）

subprocess.note: Launcher does not spawn subprocesses. The pinned npm package is loaded in-process via dynamic ESM import; see permissions.inProcessLoad.

inProcessLoad.resolution: platform-standard global node_modules roots only by default; launcher-local and CWD node_modules require BWS_ALLOW_LOCAL_INSTALL=1 (off by default)
inProcessLoad.symlinkPolicy: rejected at lstat time for both candidate iteration and require.resolve fallback
inProcessLoad.argvPolicy: allow-listed subcommands & flags; length-bounded; control-char filtered; '--' delimited; injected via process.argv mutation
inProcessLoad.preImportIntegrityGate: verifyBwsIntegrity(): package.json name + version + entry size + entry SHA-512 (timing-safe compare). No env override.
inProcessLoad.justification: In-process loader removes the exec sink from the skill repository while preserving argv validation, sensitivity-gating policy, and a hard supply-chain integrity gate.

sensitive.description: 通过 OpenClaw 在已认证的浏览器标签页中执行 JavaScript
sensitive.scope: 按 adapter 域名隔离（设计意图，非运行时强制）
risks: 第三方 npm 包在页面上下文中执行，可访问站点认证数据
恶意或被篡改的包可能窃取 cookies、私信、收藏、订单等账户数据
包代码不包含在此 Skill 中，需独立审计每个版本
Launcher 无法阻止包内 JS 访问当前 OpenClaw 中其他已打开的标签
mitigations: Launcher 对敏感 adapter 默认拒绝，需 BWS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE=1 或 --i-understand-sensitive 显式 opt-in
BWS_PUBLIC_ONLY=1 硬隔离模式可拒绝所有敏感 adapter
所有调用记录到 ~/.bws/audit.log（仅元数据）
建议为 OpenClaw 使用独立浏览器 profile，不复用日常 profile
建议 npm install 时加 --ignore-scripts 降低供应链注入面

gateOrder: Gate 2: sensitive tier sealed unless BWS_ENABLE_SENSITIVE_TIER=1
Gate 3: per-call/session opt-in via BWS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE=1 or --i-understand-sensitive

Gate 4 (v0.4.10): platform consent ledger at ~/.bws/consents.json bound to (site, pkgVersion, entrySha512); first sensitive call requires --accept-platform-consent; any drift in pkgVersion or entrySha512 invalidates prior consent
policy.auditLogContent: metadata-only (no payloads, no responses, args hashed via SHA-256); fields: ts, pid, adapter, site, primaryDomain (when known), classification, decision, reason, argHash, pkgVersion (sensitive allow records), dryRun
policy.transparencyBlock: Structured stderr line '[bws] transparency:{...}' emitted before every sensitive call and (when BWS_TRANSPARENCY=1) before every call. Names third-party pkg + audited SHA-512 + gate path + audit log path + consent ledger path. Cannot be suppressed without editing scripts/run.js.

respondsTo.quote: the core functionality inherently relies on a third-party dependency to handle sensitive session data

mitigations.name: Platform consent ledger (Gate 4)
mitigations.rationale: Even with the v0.4.4 sealed sensitive tier, a fresh package upgrade that re-passes the SHA-512 gate (after re-audit + lockstep launcher bump) would otherwise auto-allow previously consented sites. Binding consent to entrySha512 forces a deliberate re-consent on any drift.

mitigations.name: Transparency block
mitigations.rationale: A wrapping AI agent cannot silently invoke a sensitive call; downstream consumers see the third-party identity and audited bytes.

mitigations.name: --dry-run launcher flag
mitigations.behavior: Runs Gates 1-4 + integrity + symlink rejection + audit log write, then exits without importing the package. Exit 0 = would allow, non-zero = denied.
mitigations.rationale: CI / agent dispatch can confirm permission and bytes-binding without ever invoking a sensitive browser-session call.

privacyNotice.summary: 此 Skill 在你显式同意后会复用浏览器登录态，可读取你已登录站点的任何可见数据
details: 默认 sensitive tier 是封印的（v0.4.4+）；公开 adapter（hn/github/arxiv 等）无需任何 env
敏感 adapter 需要先 BWS_ENABLE_SENSITIVE_TIER=1 然后再 BWS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE=1 / --i-understand-sensitive
敏感 adapter 调用会在 stderr 打印警告，并写入 ~/.bws/audit.log（含 site / primaryDomain 字段）
访问范围取决于你在目标站点的登录权限
建议使用独立 OpenClaw 浏览器 profile，不要复用日常 profile

recommendedRuntimeProfile.rationale: Mitigates ClawScan May 2026 findings: 'Tool Misuse and Exploitation' (cross-tab containment is not enforced by the launcher), 'Identity and Privilege Abuse' (skill can read authenticated browser sessions), and the residual 'inherently relies on a third-party dependency' verdict. With this profile applied, sensitive adapters are sealed at the tier gate AND hard-isolated, AND (when re-enabled) bound to (site, pkgVersion, entrySha512) by Gate 4, AND every sensitive call emits a transparency block to stderr.

preflightChecklist: Confirm the OpenClaw browser profile is dedicated to bws (not your daily profile)
Close all tabs except the target site for the adapter you are about to call
Do not be logged in to unrelated high-value sites (banking, email, SSO) in this profile
Re-audit browser-web-search source for the pinned version before enabling the tier
First sensitive call to a site: add --accept-platform-consent (records to ~/.bws/consents.json)
Prefer running --dry-run first to confirm gate path before the real call

operatorGuidance: Treat BWS_ENABLE_SENSITIVE_TIER=1 like sudo: enable for the smallest possible scope (single shell, single agent task), not in your shell rc
Prefer per-call --i-understand-sensitive over session-wide BWS_ALLOW_SENSITIVE=1 when running ad hoc
For unattended agents, periodically diff ~/.bws/audit.log for unexpected (site, primaryDomain) tuples
Periodically review ~/.bws/consents.json; remove records for sites you no longer need authenticated access to
If you genuinely never need authenticated access, leave BWS_PUBLIC_ONLY=1 always set and treat it as the production posture
Use --dry-run in CI to validate config without invoking the package; the exit code reflects gate decisions

When upgrading browser-web-search: re-audit the new source, update REQUIRED_VERSION + ENTRY_SHA512_BASE64 + ENTRY_EXPECTED_SIZE in scripts/run.js, then re-consent (the ledger auto-invalidates on entrySha512 drift)

machineReadableContract.ifTierEnvUnset: all sensitive adapters denied at scripts/run.js:enforcePreIntegrityGates Gate 2
machineReadableContract.ifTierEnvSetAndPublicOnlyAlsoSet: PUBLIC_ONLY wins; sensitive adapters still denied at Gate 1
machineReadableContract.ifDryRunFlag: all gates run, audit record written, transparency block emitted, package NOT imported; exit 0=would-allow
